input.hypothesis: Modulating EGFR-linked mechanisms may be relevant to non-small cell lung cancer.
input.evidence_text: NCBI Gene EGFR: The protein encoded by this gene is a transmembrane glycoprotein that is a member of the protein kinase superfamily. This protein is a receptor for members of the epidermal growth factor family. EGFR is a cell surface protein that binds to epidermal growth factor, thus inducing receptor dimerization and tyrosine autophosphorylation leading to cell proliferation. Mutations in this gene are associated with lung cancer. EGFR is a component of the cytokine storm which contributes to a severe form of Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) resulting from infection with severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2). [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2020]

input.hypothesis: Modulating EGFR-linked mechanisms may be relevant to non-small cell lung cancer.
input.evidence_text: NCBI Gene MET: This gene encodes a member of the receptor tyrosine kinase family of proteins and the product of the proto-oncogene MET. The encoded preproprotein is proteolytically processed to generate alpha and beta subunits that are linked via disulfide bonds to form the mature receptor. Further processing of the beta subunit results in the formation of the M10 peptide, which has been shown to reduce lung fibrosis. Binding of its ligand, hepatocyte growth factor, induces dimerization and activation of the receptor, which plays a role in cellular survival, embryogenesis, and cellular migration and invasion. Mutations in this gene are associated with papillary renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and various head and neck cancers. Amplification and overexpression of this gene are also associated with multiple human cancers. [provided by RefSeq, May 2016]

hypothesis.title: EGFR clinical-precedence and mechanism review for non-small cell lung cancer
hypothesis.text: EGFR has established or clinically precedented target-disease grounding for non-small cell lung cancer. The output should not present this as a new target discovery. Relevant clinical literature titles include: Durvalumab Plus Chemotherapy in Patients With EGFR-Mutated Advanced NSCLC Whose Disease Progressed on First-Line Osimertinib: ORCHARD.; JIN-A02, a Mutant-Selective Fourth-Generation EGFR Inhibitor, Overcomes C797S-Mediated Resistance and Demonstrates Intracranial Activity in NSCLC.; Profiling of Extracellular Vesicles of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Reveals Proteins Associated With Osimertinib Resistance.. The remaining scientific work is to resolve mechanism details, response or resistance biology, safety liabilities, and patient-selection strategy; this is not a claim that a new target has been discovered.

evidence.text: NCBI Gene EGFR: The protein encoded by this gene is a transmembrane glycoprotein that is a member of the protein kinase superfamily. This protein is a receptor for members of the epidermal growth factor family. EGFR is a cell surface protein that binds to epidermal growth factor, thus inducing receptor dimerization and tyrosine autophosphorylation leading to cell proliferation. Mutations in this gene are associated with lung cancer. EGFR is a component of the cytokine storm which contributes to a severe form of Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) resulting from infection with severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2). [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2020]

evidence.text: NCBI Gene MET: This gene encodes a member of the receptor tyrosine kinase family of proteins and the product of the proto-oncogene MET. The encoded preproprotein is proteolytically processed to generate alpha and beta subunits that are linked via disulfide bonds to form the mature receptor. Further processing of the beta subunit results in the formation of the M10 peptide, which has been shown to reduce lung fibrosis. Binding of its ligand, hepatocyte growth factor, induces dimerization and activation of the receptor, which plays a role in cellular survival, embryogenesis, and cellular migration and invasion. Mutations in this gene are associated with papillary renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and various head and neck cancers. Amplification and overexpression of this gene are also associated with multiple human cancers. [provided by RefSeq, May 2016]

structured.text: OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: non-small cell lung carcinoma: A group of at least three distinct histological types of lung cancer, including non-small cell squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, and large cell carcinoma. hits.name: non-small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A group of at least three distinct histological types of lung cancer, including non-small cell squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, and large cell carcinoma. Non-small cell lung carcinomas have a poor response to conventional chemotherapy.

hits.name: non-small cell squamous lung carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma that arises from the lung. It is characterized by the presence of large malignant cells. It includes the clear cell and papillary variants of squamous cell carcinoma.

hits.name: lung cancer
hits.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the lung.

hits.name: large cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A poorly differentiated non-small cell lung carcinoma composed of large polygonal cells without evidence of glandular or squamous differentiation. There is a male predilection.

hits.name: combined small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of small cell lung carcinoma in combination with a non-small cell carcinoma.

hits.name: non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: Type of epithelial lung cancer arising from glandular origin.

hits.name: lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. There is a male predilection with a male to female ratio of 2:1. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor.

hits.name: Lung Sarcomatoid Carcinoma
hits.description: A rare, aggressive, poorly differentiated, non-small cell lung carcinoma characterized by the presence of a sarcomatoid component often associated with giant cell differentiation. There is a male to female ratio of 4:1. Clinical symptoms include cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, progressive dyspnea and fever secondary to recurrent pneumonia. Cigarette smoking is a major risk factor.

hits.name: methylsuccinate measurement
hits.description: Quantification of the amount of methylsuccinate in a sample.

hits.name: Optic disc pallor
hits.description: A pale yellow discoloration of the optic disc (the area of the optic nerve head in the retina). The optic disc normally has a pinkish hue with a central yellowish depression.

hits.name: cone-rod synaptic disorder syndrome, congenital nonprogressive
GCGR-related hyperglucagonemia
hits.description: A rare tumor of pancreas caused by mutations in the GCGR gene characterized by pancreatic alpha cell hyperplasia, pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors and markedly increased serum glucagon levels in the absence of a glucagonoma syndrome. Clinical manifestations may include abdominal pain, pancreatitis, fatigue, diarrhea, and diabetes mellitus.

hits.name: e3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RNF43 measurement
hits.description: The determination of the amount of e3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RNF43 in a sample

hits.name: night blindness
hits.description: Inability to see clearly in dim light.

hits.name: Hyperhidrosis
hits.description: Abnormal excessive perspiration (sweating) despite the lack of appropriate stimuli like hot and humid weather.

hits.name: disease progression measurement
hits.description: quantification of some aspect of disease progression such as speed or symptom variability. Disease progression can be quantified in a number of ways, for example as a correlated measure of progression scores from different symptom areas

hits.name: pituitary hormone deficiency, combined, 6
hits.description: Any combined pituitary hormone deficiencies, genetic form in which the cause of the disease is a mutation in the OTX2 gene.

hits.name: adult-onset non-insulinoma persistent hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia
hits.description: Diazoxide-resistant hyperinsulism (DRH) is form of congenital isolated hyperinsulinism caused by an abnormal insulin production by b-cells in the pancreas that can be diffuse or focal and is characterized by an excessive/ uncontrolled insulin secretion (inappropriate for the level of glycemia), recurrent episodes of profound hypoglycemia and resistance to medical management with diazoxide

hits.name: diabetes, deafness, developmental delay, and short stature syndrome
hypochromic anemia
hits.description: Anemia caused by the reduction of hemoglobin in relation to the red cell volume. As a result, the red cells have an area of central pallor which is increased in size. The leading cause is iron deficiency.

hits.name: benign soft tissue neoplasm
hits.description: A non-metastasizing neoplasm that arises from the soft tissue.

hits.name: level of aldehyde dehydrogenase X, mitochondrial in blood serum
hits.description: The amount of a aldehyde dehydrogenase X, mitochondrial when measured in blood serum.

hits.name: leucine-induced hypoglycemia
hits.description: Rare cause of hypoglycemia and is described as a condition in which symptomatic hypoglycemia is provoked by high protein feedings. Hypoglycemia is also elicited by administration of oral or intravenous infusions of a single amino acid, leucine. [http://www.uniprot.org/diseases/DI-01896]

hits.name: isolated growth hormone deficiency type IA
hits.description: An isolated growth hormone deficiency characterized by autosomal recessive inheritance of severe dwarfism with onset by 6 months of age and variable development of antibodies to growth hormone following exogenous supplementation that has material basis in null mutations in the GH1 gene on chromosome 17q23.3.

hits.name: small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: Small cell lung cancer (SCLC) is a highly aggressive malignant neoplasm, accounting for 10-15% of lung cancer cases, characterized byrapid growth, and early metastasis. SCLC usually manifests as a large hilar mass with bulky mediastinal lymphadenopathy presenting clinically with chest pain, persistent cough, dyspnea, wheezing, hoarseness, hemoptysis, loss of appetite, weight loss, and neurological and endocrine paraneoplastic syndromes. SCLC is primarily reported in elderly people with a history of long-term tobacco exposure.

hits.name: non-small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A group of at least three distinct histological types of lung cancer, including non-small cell squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, and large cell carcinoma. Non-small cell lung carcinomas have a poor response to conventional chemotherapy.

hits.name: combined small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of small cell lung carcinoma in combination with a non-small cell carcinoma.

hits.name: small cell carcinoma
hits.description: A neuroendocrine carcinoma composed of small malignant cells which are often said to resemble "oat cells" under the microscope. Small cell carcinoma most often affects the lungs. Clinically, this is often a rapidly growing cancer that spreads to distant sites early.

hits.name: squamous cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma arising from squamous bronchial epithelial cells. It may be keratinizing or non-keratinizing. Keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the presence of keratinization, pearl formation, and/or intercellular bridges. Non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the absence of keratinization, pearl formation, and intercellular bridges. Cigarette smoking and arsenic exposure are strongly associated with squamous cell lung carcinoma.

hits.name: Bladder Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
hits.description: A highly aggressive carcinoma, histologically resembling small cell lung carcinoma. In most cases it is associated with carcinoma in situ.

hits.name: non-small cell squamous lung carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma that arises from the lung. It is characterized by the presence of large malignant cells. It includes the clear cell and papillary variants of squamous cell carcinoma.

hits.name: squamous cell carcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma arising from squamous epithelial cells. Morphologically, it is characterized by the proliferation of atypical, often pleomorphic squamous cells. Squamous cell carcinomas are graded by the degree of cellular differentiation as well, moderately, or poorly differentiated. Well differentiated carcinomas are usually associated with keratin production and the presence of intercellular bridges between adjacent cells. Representative examples are lung squamous cell carcinoma, skin squamous cell carcinoma, and cervical squamous cell carcinoma.

hits.name: Gastric Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
hits.description: An aggressive, high-grade and poorly differentiated carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the stomach. It is characterized by the presence of malignant small cells.

hits.name: lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. There is a male predilection with a male to female ratio of 2:1. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor.

hits.name: Lung Giant Cell Carcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of lung sarcomatoid carcinoma characterized by the presence of mononuclear and multinucleated pleomorphic neoplastic giant cells that lack cohesion.

hits.name: lung carcinoid tumor
hits.description: A neuroendocrine neoplasm that arises from the lung. It is characterized by the presence of uniform polygonal cells with small or moderate amount of cytoplasm and inconspicuous nucleoli. The cells are usually arranged in organoid and trabecular patterns. It is classified as typical or atypical carcinoid tumor based on the number of mitotic figures and the absence or presence of necrosis. Atypical carcinoid tumors have a worse prognosis.

hits.name: Combined Lung Carcinoma
hits.description: A lung carcinoma characterized by the presence of large or small neuroendocrine carcinoma cells in combination with malignant glandular or squamous epithelial cells.

hits.name: pulmonary large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma
hits.description: A large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma that involves the lung(s).

hits.name: non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: Type of epithelial lung cancer arising from glandular origin.

hits.name: interstitial lung disease
hits.description: A diverse group of lung diseases that affect the lung parenchyma. They are characterized by an initial inflammation of pulmonary alveoli that extends to the interstitium and beyond leading to diffuse pulmonary fibrosis. Interstitial lung diseases are classified by their etiology (known or unknown causes), and radiological-pathological features.

hits.name: adenosquamous lung carcinoma
hits.description: An aggressive carcinoma with a poor prognosis characterized by a presence of both malignant squamous cells and glandular cells.

hits.name: lung sarcoma
hits.description: A malignant mesenchymal neoplasm that arises from the lung. Representative examples include Kaposi sarcoma, leiomyosarcoma, and synovial sarcoma.

hits.name: oral squamous cell carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma arising from the oral cavity. It affects predominantly adults in their fifth and sixth decades of life and is associated with alcohol and tobacco use. Human papillomavirus is present in approximately half of the cases. It is characterized by a tendency to metastasize early to the lymph nodes. When the tumor is small, patients are often asymptomatic. Physical examination may reveal erythematous or white lesions or plaques. The majority of patients present with signs and symptoms of locally advanced disease including mucosal ulceration, pain, difficulty with speaking, chewing, and swallowing, bleeding, weight loss, and neck swelling. Patients may also present with swollen neck lymph nodes without any symptoms from the oropharyngeal tumor. The most significant prognostic factors are the size of the tumor and the lymph nodes status.

hits.name: lung benign neoplasm
hits.description: A non-metastasizing neoplasm that arises from the lung parenchyma or the bronchi. Representative examples include lung adenoma, lung hamartoma, and endobronchial lipoma.

hits.name: squamous cell neoplasm
hits.description: A neoplasm that is composed of squamous epithelial cells. Squamous cell carcinoma is a representative example.

hits.name: lip and oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma arising from the lip or the oral cavity. The oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma usually arises from the buccal mucosa, tongue, or gums. It occurs predominantly in adults who use tobacco and alcohol and has a tendency to metastasize early to lymph nodes.

hits.name: lung colloid adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of lung adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of mucin pools containing islands of well differentiated adenocarcinoma cells.

hits.name: lung adenoma
hits.description: A benign, well circumscribed epithelial neoplasm that arises from the bronchus or the lung parenchyma. Representative examples include alveolar adenoma, papillary adenoma, and mucus gland adenoma.

hits.name: adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A common cancer characterized by the presence of malignant glandular cells. Morphologically, adenocarcinomas are classified according to the growth pattern (e.g., papillary, alveolar) or according to the secreting product (e.g., mucinous, serous). Representative examples of adenocarcinoma are ductal and lobular breast carcinoma, lung adenocarcinoma, renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma (hepatoma), colon adenocarcinoma, and prostate adenocarcinoma.

content.title: EGFR clinical-precedence and mechanism review for non-small cell lung cancer
content.hypothesis: EGFR has established or clinically precedented target-disease grounding for non-small cell lung cancer. The output should not present this as a new target discovery. Relevant clinical literature titles include: Durvalumab Plus Chemotherapy in Patients With EGFR-Mutated Advanced NSCLC Whose Disease Progressed on First-Line Osimertinib: ORCHARD.; JIN-A02, a Mutant-Selective Fourth-Generation EGFR Inhibitor, Overcomes C797S-Mediated Resistance and Demonstrates Intracranial Activity in NSCLC.; Profiling of Extracellular Vesicles of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Reveals Proteins Associated With Osimertinib Resistance.. The remaining scientific work is to resolve mechanism details, response or resistance biology, safety liabilities, and patient-selection strategy; this is not a claim that a new target has been discovered.

evidence.text: NCBI Gene EGFR: The protein encoded by this gene is a transmembrane glycoprotein that is a member of the protein kinase superfamily. This protein is a receptor for members of the epidermal growth factor family. EGFR is a cell surface protein that binds to epidermal growth factor, thus inducing receptor dimerization and tyrosine autophosphorylation leading to cell proliferation. Mutations in this gene are associated with lung cancer. EGFR is a component of the cytokine storm which contributes to a severe form of Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) resulting from infection with severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2). [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2020]

evidence.text: NCBI Gene MET: This gene encodes a member of the receptor tyrosine kinase family of proteins and the product of the proto-oncogene MET. The encoded preproprotein is proteolytically processed to generate alpha and beta subunits that are linked via disulfide bonds to form the mature receptor. Further processing of the beta subunit results in the formation of the M10 peptide, which has been shown to reduce lung fibrosis. Binding of its ligand, hepatocyte growth factor, induces dimerization and activation of the receptor, which plays a role in cellular survival, embryogenesis, and cellular migration and invasion. Mutations in this gene are associated with papillary renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and various head and neck cancers. Amplification and overexpression of this gene are also associated with multiple human cancers. [provided by RefSeq, May 2016]

hits.name: non-small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A group of at least three distinct histological types of lung cancer, including non-small cell squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, and large cell carcinoma. Non-small cell lung carcinomas have a poor response to conventional chemotherapy.

hits.name: non-small cell squamous lung carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma that arises from the lung. It is characterized by the presence of large malignant cells. It includes the clear cell and papillary variants of squamous cell carcinoma.

hits.name: lung cancer
hits.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the lung.

hits.name: large cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A poorly differentiated non-small cell lung carcinoma composed of large polygonal cells without evidence of glandular or squamous differentiation. There is a male predilection.

hits.name: combined small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of small cell lung carcinoma in combination with a non-small cell carcinoma.

hits.name: non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: Type of epithelial lung cancer arising from glandular origin.

hits.name: lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. There is a male predilection with a male to female ratio of 2:1. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor.

hits.name: Lung Sarcomatoid Carcinoma
hits.description: A rare, aggressive, poorly differentiated, non-small cell lung carcinoma characterized by the presence of a sarcomatoid component often associated with giant cell differentiation. There is a male to female ratio of 4:1. Clinical symptoms include cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, progressive dyspnea and fever secondary to recurrent pneumonia. Cigarette smoking is a major risk factor.

hits.name: methylsuccinate measurement
hits.description: Quantification of the amount of methylsuccinate in a sample.

hits.name: Optic disc pallor
hits.description: A pale yellow discoloration of the optic disc (the area of the optic nerve head in the retina). The optic disc normally has a pinkish hue with a central yellowish depression.

hits.name: cone-rod synaptic disorder syndrome, congenital nonprogressive
GCGR-related hyperglucagonemia
hits.description: A rare tumor of pancreas caused by mutations in the GCGR gene characterized by pancreatic alpha cell hyperplasia, pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors and markedly increased serum glucagon levels in the absence of a glucagonoma syndrome. Clinical manifestations may include abdominal pain, pancreatitis, fatigue, diarrhea, and diabetes mellitus.

hits.name: e3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RNF43 measurement
hits.description: The determination of the amount of e3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RNF43 in a sample

hits.name: night blindness
hits.description: Inability to see clearly in dim light.

hits.name: Hyperhidrosis
hits.description: Abnormal excessive perspiration (sweating) despite the lack of appropriate stimuli like hot and humid weather.

hits.name: disease progression measurement
hits.description: quantification of some aspect of disease progression such as speed or symptom variability. Disease progression can be quantified in a number of ways, for example as a correlated measure of progression scores from different symptom areas

hits.name: pituitary hormone deficiency, combined, 6
hits.description: Any combined pituitary hormone deficiencies, genetic form in which the cause of the disease is a mutation in the OTX2 gene.

hits.name: adult-onset non-insulinoma persistent hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia
hits.description: Diazoxide-resistant hyperinsulism (DRH) is form of congenital isolated hyperinsulinism caused by an abnormal insulin production by b-cells in the pancreas that can be diffuse or focal and is characterized by an excessive/ uncontrolled insulin secretion (inappropriate for the level of glycemia), recurrent episodes of profound hypoglycemia and resistance to medical management with diazoxide

hits.name: diabetes, deafness, developmental delay, and short stature syndrome
hypochromic anemia
hits.description: Anemia caused by the reduction of hemoglobin in relation to the red cell volume. As a result, the red cells have an area of central pallor which is increased in size. The leading cause is iron deficiency.

hits.name: benign soft tissue neoplasm
hits.description: A non-metastasizing neoplasm that arises from the soft tissue.

hits.name: level of aldehyde dehydrogenase X, mitochondrial in blood serum
hits.description: The amount of a aldehyde dehydrogenase X, mitochondrial when measured in blood serum.

hits.name: leucine-induced hypoglycemia
hits.description: Rare cause of hypoglycemia and is described as a condition in which symptomatic hypoglycemia is provoked by high protein feedings. Hypoglycemia is also elicited by administration of oral or intravenous infusions of a single amino acid, leucine. [http://www.uniprot.org/diseases/DI-01896]

hits.name: isolated growth hormone deficiency type IA
hits.description: An isolated growth hormone deficiency characterized by autosomal recessive inheritance of severe dwarfism with onset by 6 months of age and variable development of antibodies to growth hormone following exogenous supplementation that has material basis in null mutations in the GH1 gene on chromosome 17q23.3.

evidence.text: OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: small cell lung carcinoma: Small cell lung cancer (SCLC) is a highly aggressive malignant neoplasm, accounting for 10-15% of lung cancer cases, characterized byrapid growth, and early metastasis. SCLC usually manifests as a large hilar mass with bulky mediastinal lymphadenopathy presenting clinically with chest pain, persistent cough, dyspnea, wheezing, hoarseness, hemoptysis, loss of appetite, weight loss, and neurological and endocrine paraneoplastic syndromes. SCLC is primarily reported in elderly people with a history of long-term tobacco exposure.; non-small cell lung carcinoma: A group of at least three distinct histological types of lung cancer, including non-small cell squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, and large cell carcinoma. Non-small cell lung carcinomas have a poor response to conventional chemotherapy.; combined small cell lung carcinoma: A morphologic variant of small cell lung carcinoma in combination with a non-small cell carcinoma.

hits.name: small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: Small cell lung cancer (SCLC) is a highly aggressive malignant neoplasm, accounting for 10-15% of lung cancer cases, characterized byrapid growth, and early metastasis. SCLC usually manifests as a large hilar mass with bulky mediastinal lymphadenopathy presenting clinically with chest pain, persistent cough, dyspnea, wheezing, hoarseness, hemoptysis, loss of appetite, weight loss, and neurological and endocrine paraneoplastic syndromes. SCLC is primarily reported in elderly people with a history of long-term tobacco exposure.

hits.name: non-small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A group of at least three distinct histological types of lung cancer, including non-small cell squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, and large cell carcinoma. Non-small cell lung carcinomas have a poor response to conventional chemotherapy.

hits.name: combined small cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of small cell lung carcinoma in combination with a non-small cell carcinoma.

hits.name: small cell carcinoma
hits.description: A neuroendocrine carcinoma composed of small malignant cells which are often said to resemble "oat cells" under the microscope. Small cell carcinoma most often affects the lungs. Clinically, this is often a rapidly growing cancer that spreads to distant sites early.

hits.name: squamous cell lung carcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma arising from squamous bronchial epithelial cells. It may be keratinizing or non-keratinizing. Keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the presence of keratinization, pearl formation, and/or intercellular bridges. Non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the absence of keratinization, pearl formation, and intercellular bridges. Cigarette smoking and arsenic exposure are strongly associated with squamous cell lung carcinoma.

hits.name: Bladder Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
hits.description: A highly aggressive carcinoma, histologically resembling small cell lung carcinoma. In most cases it is associated with carcinoma in situ.

hits.name: non-small cell squamous lung carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma that arises from the lung. It is characterized by the presence of large malignant cells. It includes the clear cell and papillary variants of squamous cell carcinoma.

hits.name: squamous cell carcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma arising from squamous epithelial cells. Morphologically, it is characterized by the proliferation of atypical, often pleomorphic squamous cells. Squamous cell carcinomas are graded by the degree of cellular differentiation as well, moderately, or poorly differentiated. Well differentiated carcinomas are usually associated with keratin production and the presence of intercellular bridges between adjacent cells. Representative examples are lung squamous cell carcinoma, skin squamous cell carcinoma, and cervical squamous cell carcinoma.

hits.name: Gastric Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
hits.description: An aggressive, high-grade and poorly differentiated carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the stomach. It is characterized by the presence of malignant small cells.

hits.name: lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. There is a male predilection with a male to female ratio of 2:1. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor.

hits.name: Lung Giant Cell Carcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of lung sarcomatoid carcinoma characterized by the presence of mononuclear and multinucleated pleomorphic neoplastic giant cells that lack cohesion.

hits.name: lung carcinoid tumor
hits.description: A neuroendocrine neoplasm that arises from the lung. It is characterized by the presence of uniform polygonal cells with small or moderate amount of cytoplasm and inconspicuous nucleoli. The cells are usually arranged in organoid and trabecular patterns. It is classified as typical or atypical carcinoid tumor based on the number of mitotic figures and the absence or presence of necrosis. Atypical carcinoid tumors have a worse prognosis.

hits.name: Combined Lung Carcinoma
hits.description: A lung carcinoma characterized by the presence of large or small neuroendocrine carcinoma cells in combination with malignant glandular or squamous epithelial cells.

hits.name: pulmonary large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma
hits.description: A large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma that involves the lung(s).

hits.name: non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma
hits.description: Type of epithelial lung cancer arising from glandular origin.

hits.name: interstitial lung disease
hits.description: A diverse group of lung diseases that affect the lung parenchyma. They are characterized by an initial inflammation of pulmonary alveoli that extends to the interstitium and beyond leading to diffuse pulmonary fibrosis. Interstitial lung diseases are classified by their etiology (known or unknown causes), and radiological-pathological features.

hits.name: adenosquamous lung carcinoma
hits.description: An aggressive carcinoma with a poor prognosis characterized by a presence of both malignant squamous cells and glandular cells.

hits.name: lung sarcoma
hits.description: A malignant mesenchymal neoplasm that arises from the lung. Representative examples include Kaposi sarcoma, leiomyosarcoma, and synovial sarcoma.

hits.name: oral squamous cell carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma arising from the oral cavity. It affects predominantly adults in their fifth and sixth decades of life and is associated with alcohol and tobacco use. Human papillomavirus is present in approximately half of the cases. It is characterized by a tendency to metastasize early to the lymph nodes. When the tumor is small, patients are often asymptomatic. Physical examination may reveal erythematous or white lesions or plaques. The majority of patients present with signs and symptoms of locally advanced disease including mucosal ulceration, pain, difficulty with speaking, chewing, and swallowing, bleeding, weight loss, and neck swelling. Patients may also present with swollen neck lymph nodes without any symptoms from the oropharyngeal tumor. The most significant prognostic factors are the size of the tumor and the lymph nodes status.

hits.name: lung benign neoplasm
hits.description: A non-metastasizing neoplasm that arises from the lung parenchyma or the bronchi. Representative examples include lung adenoma, lung hamartoma, and endobronchial lipoma.

hits.name: squamous cell neoplasm
hits.description: A neoplasm that is composed of squamous epithelial cells. Squamous cell carcinoma is a representative example.

hits.name: lip and oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma
hits.description: A squamous cell carcinoma arising from the lip or the oral cavity. The oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma usually arises from the buccal mucosa, tongue, or gums. It occurs predominantly in adults who use tobacco and alcohol and has a tendency to metastasize early to lymph nodes.

hits.name: lung colloid adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A morphologic variant of lung adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of mucin pools containing islands of well differentiated adenocarcinoma cells.

hits.name: lung adenoma
hits.description: A benign, well circumscribed epithelial neoplasm that arises from the bronchus or the lung parenchyma. Representative examples include alveolar adenoma, papillary adenoma, and mucus gland adenoma.

hits.name: adenocarcinoma
hits.description: A common cancer characterized by the presence of malignant glandular cells. Morphologically, adenocarcinomas are classified according to the growth pattern (e.g., papillary, alveolar) or according to the secreting product (e.g., mucinous, serous). Representative examples of adenocarcinoma are ductal and lobular breast carcinoma, lung adenocarcinoma, renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma (hepatoma), colon adenocarcinoma, and prostate adenocarcinoma.

case_profile.objective: Analyze a complex acquired-resistance scenario after first-line osimertinib in EGFR-mutant NSCLC and produce a ranked, audit-ready research strategy rather than a treatment recommendation. Synthesize evidence on: (1) on-target resistance through EGFR C797S, EGFR amplification, secondary EGFR alterations, and T790M/C797S cis-versus-trans clone phasing; (2) bypass resistance via MET amplification, HER2/ERBB2 amplification, RET or ALK fusion emergence, BRAF V600E, KRAS/NRAS alterations, and PI3K pathway activation; (3) cell-state and lineage changes including AXL-high EMT-like transition, small-cell transformation, and squamous transformation; (4) anatomical or pharmacologic resistance contexts, including CNS sanctuary progression and exposure-limited resistance hypotheses; (5) fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors and rational combination strategies under clinical evaluation, with explicit safety and trial-design caveats; (6) biomarker and cohort validation using ctDNA, paired pre/post-treatment biopsy, copy-number profiling, fusion detection, single-cell or spatial assays where relevant; and (7) ranked next experiments distinguishing driver resistance mechanisms from bystander alterations, with controls, failure gates, and confidence-update criteria.

calibration_notes: Reviewer critique severity `medium` reduced readiness by 10 points.

gaps.rationale: The claim graph still has unsupported evidence links: PubMed: bypass resistance HER2 RET ALK BRAF osimertinib EGFR mutant lung cancer genomic profiling, PubMed: fourth generation EGFR inhibitor BLU-945 BBT-176 clinical trial resistance C797S, Reactome: EGFR.
gaps.needed_evidence: claim-specific supporting evidence or explicit claim softening
follow_up_queries: EGFR non-small cell lung cancer causal evidence
gaps.experiment_implication: Repair unsupported claim links or soften the hypothesis boundary.

scientist_positions.position: EGFR is a fully established therapeutic target in NSCLC; the unresolved scientific work concerns mechanistic stratification of acquired resistance after first-line osimertinib and rational selection of subsequent interventions. Literature supports a ranked experimental agenda rather than any new target claim.
key_claims: On-target resistance (C797S, EGFR amplification, T790M/C797S cis-trans phasing) is the best-characterized mechanism class and should anchor the primary experimental hierarchy

Bypass resistance mechanisms (MET/HER2 amplification, RET/ALK fusions, BRAF V600E, PI3K activation) are clinically documented but require paired biopsy and ctDNA phasing to distinguish driver from bystander

Lineage plasticity (SCLC transformation, AXL-high EMT, squamous shift) represents a mechanistically distinct resistance class requiring histologic re-biopsy and single-cell or spatial transcriptomics for validation

Fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors (e.g., JIN-A02) show preclinical and early clinical C797S activity with intracranial penetration, but safety and combination trial data remain immature per PMID 41649868
supporting_evidence_sources: PMID 42170254: comprehensive resistance landscape review covering on-target and bypass mechanisms with mechanistic detail
PMID 41649868: JIN-A02 fourth-generation inhibitor preclinical/early clinical data including CNS activity against C797S
PMID 41780641 and 42039685: ORCHARD platform trial arms providing clinical response data stratified by post-osimertinib resistance context
OpenTargets EGFR association score 0.888 for NSCLC confirming established target-disease grounding; ERBB2/ALK/KRAS scores 0.81-0.84 supporting bypass target relevance
concerns: Most resistance mechanism frequency data derive from retrospective or single-arm cohorts with heterogeneous biopsy timing, limiting causal inference
ctDNA-only profiling cannot resolve cis-versus-trans clone phasing for T790M/C797S without single-molecule or single-cell confirmation, risking misclassification of actionable subclones
Lineage transformation prevalence is likely underestimated due to biopsy underperformance; spatial or single-cell assays are rarely paired with matched pre-treatment tissue in published series

Fourth-generation inhibitor combination safety data are absent or immature; extrapolating efficacy from C797S cell lines to heterogeneous clinical resistance landscapes is a high-confidence failure mode
requested_followups: Require paired pre/post-osimertinib biopsy with concurrent ctDNA for all resistance mechanism frequency claims to separate driver from bystander alterations
Mandate single-molecule phasing (e.g., long-read sequencing or ddPCR allele linkage) for any T790M/C797S cis-trans classification before therapeutic stratification
Include histologic re-biopsy with IHC/RNA panels (neuroendocrine markers, AXL, p63/p40) as a failure gate before attributing progression solely to kinase-domain resistance
Specify CNS pharmacokinetic endpoints and intracranial response criteria as co-primary in any fourth-generation inhibitor trial given documented CNS sanctuary progression on osimertinib

scientist_positions.position: OpenTargets and PubMed records confirm EGFR as a top-scored target (0.888) in NSCLC with extensive clinical precedence; the scientific task is resistance mechanism resolution and patient-selection strategy, not target discovery.
key_claims: EGFR C797S and EGFR amplification are the dominant on-target post-osimertinib resistance mechanisms with fourth-generation inhibitors (JIN-A02) showing preclinical/early clinical activity
MET amplification and ERBB2 amplification are the most actionable bypass mechanisms with combinatorial trial evidence in ORCHARD
AXL-high EMT and small-cell transformation represent lineage-plasticity resistance requiring orthogonal assays beyond standard ctDNA
Ranked experiment priority: ctDNA clone phasing (C797S cis/trans) > paired biopsy with CNV > fusion panel > single-cell lineage markers
supporting_evidence_sources: OpenTargets EGFR association score 0.888 for NSCLC (strong_support)
PMID 41649868 JIN-A02 fourth-gen EGFR inhibitor C797S and CNS activity
PMID 42039685 ORCHARD durvalumab+chemo post-osimertinib
PMID 42170254 resistance landscape TKI NSCLC mechanisms review
concerns: C797S cis/trans phasing data are largely preclinical; clinical clone-phasing cohorts are small and may not generalize
Lineage transformation frequency is underestimated without systematic re-biopsy protocols; ctDNA alone insufficient
Fourth-generation inhibitor safety profiles (cardiac, skin, GI) not yet characterized in large trials; no head-to-head data
Bypass mechanism co-occurrence rates vary widely across studies, limiting single-mechanism targeting strategies
requested_followups: Retrieve OpenTargets drug evidence for MET and ERBB2 in post-osimertinib NSCLC to rank combination partners
Query ORCHARD sub-cohort outcomes stratified by resistance mechanism to validate biomarker-selection framework
Obtain single-cell or spatial transcriptomic dataset references for AXL-high EMT frequency post-osimertinib
Confirm CNS pharmacokinetic data for JIN-A02 versus osimertinib to assess sanctuary-site hypothesis

scientist_positions.position: EGFR-mutant NSCLC acquired resistance after first-line osimertinib involves heterogeneous on-target, bypass, and lineage-plasticity mechanisms requiring mechanism-stratified research strategies rather than uniform salvage approaches
key_claims: C797S in cis with T790M confers full osimertinib resistance while trans configuration retains partial sensitivity to EGFR inhibitor combinations
MET amplification and ERBB2 amplification are the dominant bypass mechanisms each occurring in ~10-15% of post-osimertinib progressors and are actionable with approved agents
Small-cell and squamous lineage transformations represent irreversible cell-state changes requiring histologic re-biopsy for detection and distinct therapeutic strategies
Fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors like JIN-A02 show preclinical and early clinical activity against C797S but CNS penetrance and combination safety data remain immature
ctDNA clone phasing for cis/trans C797S determination is technically feasible but requires orthogonal tissue confirmation before treatment decisions
supporting_evidence_sources: PubMed PMID 41649868 JIN-A02 fourth-generation EGFR inhibitor C797S intracranial activity
PubMed PMID 42170254 resistance landscape EGFR TKIs molecular mechanisms review
OpenTargets EGFR association score 0.888 NSCLC KRAS 0.842 ALK 0.812 ERBB2 0.811
PubMed PMID 42039685 ORCHARD durvalumab chemotherapy post-osimertinib progression
concerns: Biopsy sampling bias: single-site biopsy misses spatial heterogeneity of co-occurring resistance mechanisms in polyclonal progression
ctDNA allele phasing for cis/trans C797S has limited sensitivity at low VAF and may misclassify mixed clonal populations
Fourth-generation inhibitor safety data are from small early-phase cohorts; cardiac and skin toxicity profiles not yet characterized at therapeutic doses
Lineage transformation frequency may be underestimated due to inadequate re-biopsy rates in routine practice
requested_followups: Paired pre/post-osimertinib biopsy with single-cell RNA-seq to distinguish driver resistance clones from bystander alterations using clonal trajectory analysis
Prospective ctDNA cis/trans phasing study with orthogonal ddPCR validation and clinical outcome correlation
MET FISH plus ERBB2 IHC/FISH on all post-osimertinib progression biopsies as mandatory stratification in combination trial designs
CNS pharmacokinetic studies for fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors in patient-derived xenograft intracranial models before expansion cohort enrollment
scientist_positions.discipline: disease mechanism and causal pathway biology

scientist_positions.position: EGFR is a well-validated, clinically precedented target in NSCLC; the scientific work is resolving post-osimertinib resistance mechanisms and rational next-line strategies, not rediscovering the target.
key_claims: C797S is the dominant on-target resistance mutation after osimertinib; cis vs trans phasing with T790M dictates fourth-gen inhibitor eligibility
Bypass resistance via MET/HER2 amplification and fusion emergence (ALK/RET) accounts for ~30-40% of cases and requires combination or switch strategies
Cell-state transitions (SCLC transformation, AXL-high EMT) represent non-genomic resistance requiring orthogonal biomarkers beyond ctDNA point mutations
JIN-A02 and related fourth-generation allosteric inhibitors show preclinical C797S activity and intracranial penetration but lack mature clinical efficacy/safety data
Biomarker-stratified cohort design with paired pre/post biopsy plus ctDNA is prerequisite for any ranked resistance-mechanism trial
supporting_evidence_sources: PubMed PMID 41649868: JIN-A02 fourth-gen EGFR inhibitor C797S activity and CNS data
PubMed PMID 42170254: resistance landscape review covering on-target and bypass mechanisms
PubMed PMID 42039685: ORCHARD durvalumab+chemo post-osimertinib cohort
OpenTargets EGFR association score 0.888 in NSCLC confirming target-disease grounding
concerns: C797S cis/trans phasing data are largely preclinical or small retrospective series; prospective validation is absent
SCLC and squamous transformation frequencies post-osimertinib are underestimated without systematic re-biopsy protocols
Fourth-generation inhibitor safety profiles (cardiac, skin, GI) are not yet characterized in adequately powered trials
ctDNA alone may miss focal CNS or lineage-switch resistance requiring tissue or CSF sampling
requested_followups: Prospective paired tissue+ctDNA re-biopsy study with allele-phasing assay to quantify C797S cis vs trans frequency
Single-cell or spatial transcriptomic profiling of post-osimertinib biopsies to enumerate AXL-high EMT and SCLC-transformed subclones
CNS pharmacokinetic study of fourth-generation inhibitors with CSF drug-level correlation to intracranial response
Basket trial with pre-specified failure gates per resistance mechanism stratum (MET amp, HER2 amp, C797S, transformation)

scientist_positions.position: As safety_agent, I challenge overclaims in the resistance-mechanism hypothesis and flag translational risks requiring explicit evidence gates before clinical application.
key_claims: C797S/T790M cis-vs-trans phasing determines fourth-gen inhibitor eligibility but phasing data from ctDNA remain technically unreliable at low VAF
Bypass resistance mechanisms (MET, HER2 amp) are heterogeneous and co-occurring; single-agent targeting without co-resistance profiling risks partial response and accelerated clonal selection
Small-cell and squamous transformation are histologic diagnoses requiring tissue biopsy; ctDNA alone cannot confirm lineage switch and acting on liquid biopsy alone is unsafe

Fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors (e.g., JIN-A02) show preclinical and early-phase CNS activity but lack randomized safety data; toxicity profiles at therapeutic CNS-penetrant doses are uncharacterized
supporting_evidence_sources: PMID 41649868 JIN-A02 preclinical/early-phase C797S activity
PMID 42039685 ORCHARD durvalumab+chemo post-osimertinib
PMID 42170254 resistance landscape review
OpenTargets EGFR NSCLC association score 0.888
concerns: Cis/trans phasing by ctDNA has high false-negative rate at VAF<1%; clinical decisions based on phasing without orthogonal tissue confirmation risk misclassification
Bypass resistance co-occurrence rates are underreported; combination strategies targeting single bypass driver assume clonal homogeneity not supported by single-cell data
Lineage transformation frequency may be underestimated due to biopsy site selection bias; CNS-only progression may harbor divergent histology
No head-to-head safety comparison exists between fourth-gen EGFR inhibitors and standard post-osimertinib regimens; ORCHARD arms are non-randomized
requested_followups: Require paired tissue biopsy plus ctDNA with allele phasing confirmation before enrolling C797S patients in fourth-gen inhibitor trials
Mandate multi-region or single-cell profiling in at least one resistance cohort to quantify bypass co-occurrence and clonal architecture
Define explicit failure gates: if ORR<20% or grade3+ toxicity>30% in phase1 expansion, halt combination arm
Establish CNS-specific biopsy or CSF ctDNA sub-study to characterize sanctuary-site resistance mechanisms independently

scientist_positions.position: Pathway and cellular evidence strongly support the established EGFR oncogenic driver role in NSCLC; the scientific work now centers on resolving heterogeneous post-osimertinib resistance mechanisms and rational combination strategies
key_claims: EGFR C797S is the dominant on-target resistance mutation after osimertinib; cis vs trans phasing with T790M determines fourth-generation inhibitor eligibility
Bypass resistance via MET/HER2 amplification and lineage plasticity (SCLC transformation, AXL-high EMT) are mechanistically distinct and require orthogonal therapeutic strategies
Fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors (e.g., JIN-A02) show preclinical/early clinical activity against C797S and intracranial disease but lack mature efficacy and safety data
ctDNA clone phasing and paired biopsy are essential to distinguish driver from bystander alterations and to stratify patients for mechanism-matched trials
supporting_evidence_sources: OpenTargets EGFR-NSCLC association score 0.888 confirms established target-disease link
PubMed PMID 41649868: JIN-A02 fourth-gen inhibitor overcomes C797S and shows CNS activity
PubMed PMID 42170254: resistance landscape review covering on-target and bypass mechanisms
ORCHARD platform trial PMIDs 42039685 and 41780641 provide biomarker-stratified post-osimertinib combination data
concerns: Clone phasing data from ctDNA may miss spatial heterogeneity captured only by multi-region biopsy
Lineage transformation (SCLC) frequency may be underestimated without systematic re-biopsy protocols
Fourth-generation inhibitor safety profiles (cardiac, skin, GI toxicity) are not yet fully characterized in large cohorts
Bypass resistance mechanisms are often co-occurring, complicating single-agent combination logic
requested_followups: Mandate paired pre/post-osimertinib biopsy with single-cell RNA-seq to distinguish EMT-like state from true lineage transformation
Perform allele-specific PCR or long-read sequencing on ctDNA to phase C797S with T790M in cis vs trans
Establish isogenic cell-line panels with defined resistance alleles to test fourth-generation inhibitor combinations with MET or HER2 inhibitors under controlled conditions
Define failure gates for each resistance subtype experiment: e.g., >50% growth inhibition at clinically achievable drug concentrations as minimum bar

scientist_positions.position: The hypothesis card correctly frames EGFR as an established clinical target in NSCLC and appropriately scopes remaining work to resistance mechanism resolution, patient selection, and combination strategy. This framing is scientifically sound but the evidence digest and hypothesis card leave several critical analytical gaps that must be addressed before a ranked research strategy can be considered audit-ready.
key_claims: C797S and bypass mechanisms (MET, HER2 amplification) are the dominant post-osimertinib resistance drivers but their relative frequencies are not quantified in the card
Fourth-generation inhibitors like JIN-A02 show preclinical C797S activity but clinical validation is nascent and CNS data are preliminary
ORCHARD cohort data support biomarker-stratified combination approaches but response rates and durability remain immature
Lineage plasticity (SCLC transformation, EMT) is mechanistically distinct and requires orthogonal detection strategies not addressed in the card
supporting_evidence_sources: PubMed PMID 42170254: resistance landscape review covering on-target and bypass mechanisms
PubMed PMID 41649868: JIN-A02 fourth-generation inhibitor C797S and intracranial activity
PubMed PMID 42039685: ORCHARD durvalumab plus chemotherapy post-osimertinib
OpenTargets EGFR association score 0.888 for NSCLC confirming target-disease grounding
concerns: No clone-phasing data (cis vs trans T790M/C797S) cited; this distinction is critical for fourth-generation inhibitor eligibility and is absent from the evidence digest
Bypass resistance frequencies (MET amplification ~15-20%, HER2 ~5%) are not quantified in the card, making prioritization of combination experiments unjustified without this baseline
Lineage transformation detection requires tissue biopsy; ctDNA alone is insufficient, yet the card does not specify biopsy requirements or failure gates for liquid-only profiling

ORCHARD data cited are from a non-randomized platform trial with small biomarker-selected cohorts; extrapolation to ranked strategy requires explicit acknowledgment of selection bias and immature follow-up
requested_followups: Provide clone-phasing data or cite studies distinguishing cis vs trans C797S/T790M to justify fourth-generation inhibitor prioritization
Quantify bypass resistance mechanism frequencies from prospective post-osimertinib biopsy cohorts (e.g., APPLE, FLAURA2 resistance substudies) to rank combination experiment priority
Specify tissue biopsy requirements and failure gates for lineage transformation detection that cannot be resolved by ctDNA alone
Define confidence-update criteria: what ctDNA VAF threshold or copy-number cutoff would reclassify a mechanism from driver to bystander in the proposed experiments

agreements: EGFR is an established therapeutic target in NSCLC; the scientific task is resistance mechanism resolution, not target discovery
On-target resistance (C797S, EGFR amplification, T790M/C797S cis/trans phasing) is the best-characterized mechanism class and should anchor the experimental hierarchy
MET and ERBB2 amplification are the most clinically actionable bypass mechanisms, each occurring in approximately 10-15% of post-osimertinib progressors
Lineage plasticity (SCLC transformation, AXL-high EMT, squamous shift) requires histologic re-biopsy and orthogonal assays beyond ctDNA alone
Fourth-generation inhibitors (e.g., JIN-A02) show preclinical and early clinical C797S activity with intracranial penetration, but safety and combination data remain immature
ctDNA alone is insufficient for cis-versus-trans T790M/C797S clone phasing; single-molecule or single-cell confirmation is required
disagreements: Agents differ implicitly on experiment ranking order: literature_agent prioritizes cis/trans phasing first, tooluniverse_agent places ctDNA clone phasing first but adds CNV and fusion panel as co-equal second steps, while the third agent does not provide an explicit ranked list beyond the four mechanism classes

No explicit disagreement on mechanism frequencies, but agents vary in confidence about whether 10-15% bypass mechanism estimates are generalizable given heterogeneous biopsy timing across source cohorts
overclaims: Stating C797S cis/trans phasing has clear therapeutic stratification implications overstates current clinical evidence; supporting data are largely preclinical or from small cohorts
Asserting JIN-A02 'demonstrates intracranial activity' as a settled finding overstates early-phase data; CNS pharmacokinetic and efficacy endpoints are not yet validated in adequately powered trials
Bypass mechanism co-occurrence frequency estimates (10-15%) are presented with more precision than retrospective, heterogeneous-biopsy-timing cohorts can support
Lineage transformation prevalence figures should not be cited as reliable estimates given systematic underperformance of re-biopsy protocols in published series
required_revisions: All resistance mechanism frequency claims must specify biopsy timing, cohort design (prospective vs. retrospective), and whether ctDNA or tissue was used, to allow causal inference assessment
T790M/C797S cis/trans classification must require single-molecule phasing confirmation (long-read sequencing or ddPCR allele linkage) before any therapeutic stratification claim is made
Fourth-generation inhibitor efficacy and safety statements must include explicit trial-maturity caveats and distinguish preclinical from early-phase clinical evidence
Lineage transformation frequency estimates must be flagged as lower bounds pending systematic paired pre/post-biopsy protocols with IHC/RNA neuroendocrine and EMT marker panels
CNS sanctuary progression claims must specify whether intracranial response criteria and PK endpoints were co-primary or exploratory in cited studies

pi_adjudication.rationale: {"accepted_claims":["EGFR is an established therapeutic target in NSCLC; the scientific task is resistance mechanism resolution, not target discovery","On-target resistance (C797S, EGFR amplification, T790M/C797S cis/trans phasing) is the best-characterized mechanism class and should anchor the experimental hierarchy","MET and ERBB2 amplification are clinically documented bypass mechanisms occurring in a minority of post-osimertinib progressors and are actionable with approved agents","Lineage plasticity (SCLC transformation, AXL-high EMT, squamous shift) requires histologic re-biopsy and orthogonal assays beyond ctDNA alone","ctDNA alone is insufficient for cis-versus-trans T790M/C797S clone phasing; single-molecule or single-cell confirmation is required"],"softened_or_rejected_claims":["Bypass mechanism frequency estimates of 10-15% are softened: these figures derive from retrospective cohorts with heterogeneous biopsy timing and should be treated as provisional ranges rather than precise estimates","JIN-A02 intracranial activity claim is softened: early-phase signals are promising but CNS pharmacokinetic and efficacy endpoints are not yet validated in adequately powered trials"

actionability_profile.interpretation: disease-specific intervention or clinical precedence evidence is present; support is allowed with limits

contradictions.source: openFDA adverse events: osimertinib
contradictions.text: openFDA returned 7990 matching adverse-event reports. Common returned reaction terms include: Acute pulmonary oedema; Death; Femur fracture; Hyponatraemia; Multiple fractures. These are safety signals, not incidence rates or causal proof.

key_claims: EGFR holds an Open Targets association score of 0.888 for non-small cell lung carcinoma versus 0.468 for squamous NSCLC, indicating that clinical evidence is substantially stronger in non-squamous NSCLC subtypes.

The tertiary EGFR mutation C797S is a documented on-target resistance mechanism to osimertinib, and the fourth-generation inhibitor JIN-A02 demonstrates preclinical mutant-selective activity against C797S with intracranial penetration in NSCLC models.

The ORCHARD platform trial has evaluated post-osimertinib regimens including durvalumab plus chemotherapy and osimertinib plus datopotamab deruxtecan in EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC patients who progressed on first-line osimertinib.

EGFR-activating mutations drive downstream ERK, AKT, and STAT3 signaling, and triple mutations including L792H and G796R represent additional resistance variants that retain sensitivity to investigational agents such as cudratricusxanthone A in preclinical studies.

critique.critique: The hypothesis card correctly frames EGFR as a clinically precedented target in NSCLC and avoids new-discovery overclaiming. However, several evidence links remain unsupported in the claim graph (bypass resistance genomic profiling, fourth-generation inhibitor BLU-945/BBT-176 clinical data, Reactome EGFR pathway). The MET gene evidence is included without clear justification for its role in the primary EGFR hypothesis, risking scope inflation. Safety evidence from openFDA is present but not explicitly mapped to specific patient-selection or contraindication claims. The EGFR association score of 0.468 in squamous NSCLC versus 0.888 in non-squamous is not reconciled, potentially overgeneralizing across NSCLC subtypes. Evidence hierarchy for NCBI Gene entries is classified as tier_unknown with low confidence, weakening mechanistic grounding.
critique.recommended_fix: Explicitly reconcile EGFR association differences between squamous and non-squamous NSCLC subtypes in the hypothesis boundary. Remove or justify MET evidence inclusion. Map openFDA safety signals to specific patient-selection exclusion criteria. Repair unsupported claim graph links by adding targeted PubMed queries for BLU-945/BBT-176 and bypass resistance mechanisms. Upgrade NCBI Gene evidence tier classification using curated database sources.
critique.claim_boundary: Hypothesis should be bounded to EGFR-mutated non-squamous NSCLC as the primary indication with explicit acknowledgment that squamous NSCLC evidence is weaker; resistance biology and patient-selection strategy remain open scientific questions not resolved by current evidence.

case_profile.objective: Analyze a complex acquired-resistance scenario after first-line osimertinib in EGFR-mutant NSCLC and produce a ranked, audit-ready research strategy rather than a treatment recommendation. Synthesize evidence on: (1) on-target resistance through EGFR C797S, EGFR amplification, secondary EGFR alterations, and T790M/C797S cis-versus-trans clone phasing; (2) bypass resistance via MET amplification, HER2/ERBB2 amplification, RET or ALK fusion emergence, BRAF V600E, KRAS/NRAS alterations, and PI3K pathway activation; (3) cell-state and lineage changes including AXL-high EMT-like transition, small-cell transformation, and squamous transformation; (4) anatomical or pharmacologic resistance contexts, including CNS sanctuary progression and exposure-limited resistance hypotheses; (5) fourth-generation EGFR inhibitors and rational combination strategies under clinical evaluation, with explicit safety and trial-design caveats; (6) biomarker and cohort validation using ctDNA, paired pre/post-treatment biopsy, copy-number profiling, fusion detection, single-cell or spatial assays where relevant; and (7) ranked next experiments distinguishing driver resistance mechanisms from bystander alterations, with controls, failure gates, and confidence-update criteria.

steps.purpose: Collect biomedical tool evidence with provenance.
Use public biomedical evidence, Open Targets context, and explicit validation experiments for cell-context and omics-aware reasoning.
Run therapeutic reasoning over drug, contraindication, interaction, and safety context when configured.
Attach every claim to supporting, contradictory, or missing evidence.
Show which case-specific evidence requirements are covered, partial, or missing.
Rank proposed experiments by decision impact, feasibility, controls, and failure criteria.
Publish hypotheses, critiques, revisions, and provenance to the research board.

evaluation_criteria.criterion: Identifies the primary biomedical entities and objective scope.
Separates direct evidence from indirect or planning-only evidence.
States uncertainty and avoids unsupported clinical efficacy or safety claims.
Includes limitations and concrete validation experiments.
Reviews intervention-specific safety, contraindication, and translation gaps.
Checks omics context, cell type, dataset compatibility, and analysis validity.
Explains target-disease relevance without overstating causality.
Answers the specific user objective rather than a generic biomedical prompt.

contradictions.text: openFDA returned 7990 matching adverse-event reports. Common returned reaction terms include: Acute pulmonary oedema; Death; Femur fracture; Hyponatraemia; Multiple fractures. These are safety signals, not incidence rates or causal proof.

contradictions.source: openFDA adverse events: amivantamab
contradictions.text: openFDA returned 1021 matching adverse-event reports. Common returned reaction terms include: Deep vein thrombosis; Dizziness; Fatigue; Hepatic function abnormal; Pneumothorax. These are safety signals, not incidence rates or causal proof.

key_claims: EGFR holds an Open Targets association score of 0.888 for non-small cell lung carcinoma versus 0.468 for squamous NSCLC, indicating that clinical evidence is substantially stronger in non-squamous NSCLC subtypes.

The tertiary EGFR mutation C797S is a documented on-target resistance mechanism to osimertinib, and the fourth-generation inhibitor JIN-A02 demonstrates preclinical mutant-selective activity against C797S with intracranial penetration in NSCLC models.

The ORCHARD platform trial has evaluated post-osimertinib regimens including durvalumab plus chemotherapy and osimertinib plus datopotamab deruxtecan in EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC patients who progressed on first-line osimertinib.

EGFR-activating mutations drive downstream ERK, AKT, and STAT3 signaling, and triple mutations including L792H and G796R represent additional resistance variants that retain sensitivity to investigational agents such as cudratricusxanthone A in preclinical studies.
guardrails: Target-disease or clinical-precedence evidence must be separated from efficacy and safety claims.
Requires experimental validation before clinical interpretation.
